title: Check out the issue!
description: Join this undersea party with sharks! From curious great white sharks to seagrass snacking bonnetheads (relatives of hammerheads) to gentle—and giant!—whale sharks, the more than 500 species of sharks are full of surprises. In this issue, you'll meet seven species with amazing attributes before playing a personality quiz to discover which shark would be your bestie. Can't get enough shark stuff? Grab a parent and check out SharkFest on Disney+ starting July 6.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: It's panda-monium! This month's cover animal is giant panda Bao Li (BOW lee), who traveled across the globe with his companion, Qing Bao (ching BOW). The pair flew on the "Panda Express" from their home in China to their new digs at the Smithsonian’s National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute in Washington, D.C. Get a behind-the-scenes look at their epic adventure—plus, see how they're settling in at their new home. (Spoiler alert: They're getting lots of treats!)
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: So. Many. Penguins! Journey to Antarctica to see the moment when a group of emperor penguin chicks jump into the ocean for their first swim. But while most emperor chicks jump about two feet into the water, these birds are making a 50-foot plunge! Then travel to islands in the Pacific Ocean where you'll meet some sneaky Galápagos penguins stealing fish from pelicans. Plus, show your smarts by taking an all-about-penguins quiz.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Dive beneath the ocean waves to get a never-before-seen look at the wreckage of the R.M.S. Titanic. You'll see incredible 3D scans of the doomed ship, thanks to cutting-edge technology that scientists are using for research. Plus, an illustrated graphic shows how experts—including filmmaker and National Geographic Explorer at Large James Cameron—think the Titanic met its watery grave.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Uncover the wild world of insects with Nat Geo Kids to celebrate the second season of A Real Bug's Life, on Disney+ now! You'll meet eight incredible insects that earned our silly superlatives, from the creepy-crawly with the coolest glow to the bug with the funniest name. Then, play a fun maze game to help a yellow garden spider get from one side of its web to the butterfly trapped in the middle. Keep scrolling for more un-bee-lievable features in this issue.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Discover how snowy owl chicks grow from helpless hatchlings to stealthy predators in just three months. These powerful birds have some secret weapons to help them hunt: Their huge eyes work like binoculars to spot scurrying prey from far away, even at night; plus, their feathers have fringe that quiets the noise from flapping wings.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: One of the most successful predators on the planet, European red foxes use their impressive hunting skills to catch prey as varied as mice, frogs, fish, waterfowl, and earthworms. You'll discover how these foxes swim, climb, and listen very carefully to find food. Plus, you'll meet a fox named Roxy who regularly visits a wildlife photographer's home in Hungary.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Journey down the Amazon River with Nat Geo Explorers! Along the way, you'll track Andean bears by searching for scat, escape a sinking boat (and a river full of black caimans!), meet some helpful pink river dolphins, and release thousands of South American river turtle hatchlings back into the wild. Think you have what it takes for this expedition? Take a quiz to answer questions that every explorer ought to know.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Discover how scientists assembled the massive skeleton of a newly discovered dino and see photos of what experts think it might have looked like. Then explore the tools archaeologists used at the site to unearth the fragile discovery. And be sure to play "Dino Superstars" to find out which dino might win the award for most dangerous, loudest, smallest, and more!
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Discover why spotted hyenas are the supermoms of the animal world. These females are fierce, but they're also gentle mamas and care for their cubs longer than any other African predator. And see the special remote-controlled robot camera used to safely photograph hyenas in the wild.
author: Unknown

title: Check out the issue!
description: Do animals play? Sometimes it’s hard to tell if an animal’s behavior means it’s defending territory or finding food—or just having fun. Read stories about tricky fennec foxes, silly parrots, splashy tigers, and other wild critters, then decide for yourself what they're up to.
author: Unknown

title: Science bloopers
description: Even scientists goof up sometimes! These Nat Geo Explorers share some of their wildest slipups and hardest fails.
author: Allyson Shaw

title: Spotting fake news
description: Can you believe everything you read? Not always. Sometimes it’s hard to tell the difference between real-life headlines and made-up ones—especially on the Internet. And some people try to trick you on purpose. Follow these tips for sniffing out fake news.
author: Kay Boatner

title: Cool inventions
description: These 10 supersmart gadgets, robots, and vehicles could change your life!
author: C.M. Tomlin
